questions.stimulus: A company develops a voice recognition system for customer service calls. The development team consists entirely of native English speakers from the same region of the United States.
questions.question: Which of the following is the most likely consequence of the team's composition?

options.A: The system will not be able to process audio input because the team lacked audio engineers
options.B: The system may perform poorly for users with different accents or dialects because those perspectives were not represented during development
options.C: The system will work correctly for all users because voice recognition technology is accent-neutral
options.D: The system will contain more syntax errors because a homogeneous team communicates less effectively

questions.explanation: Collaboration that includes diverse perspectives helps avoid bias. A team of native English speakers from one region is unlikely to anticipate the needs of users with different accents or dialects, resulting in a biased system.

questions.stimulus: A company develops a voice recognition system for customer service calls. The development team consists entirely of native English speakers from the same region of the United States.
questions.question: Which of the following actions would MOST directly address the limitation described?

options.A: Expanding the team to include more native English speakers from other regions
options.B: Consulting with potential users who speak different languages and dialects during development
options.C: Using pair programming to have two developers review all code simultaneously
options.D: Using online collaboration tools so team members can share feedback more efficiently

questions.explanation: Information gathered from potential users can be used to understand the purpose of a program from diverse perspectives. Consulting users who speak different languages and dialects would directly address the bias in the system.

questions.question: A student is classifying the following items as computing innovations or not: a smart insulin pump, a printed cookbook, and a ride-sharing service. Which of the following correctly classifies all three?

options.A: All three are computing innovations because they all help people accomplish tasks
options.B: Only the smart insulin pump is a computing innovation because it is the only physical device with embedded technology
options.C: The smart insulin pump and ride-sharing service are computing innovations; the printed cookbook is not
options.D: The ride-sharing service is not a computing innovation because it involves human drivers rather than automated systems

questions.explanation: A computing innovation must include a program as an integral part of its function. A smart insulin pump and ride-sharing service both require programs to function. A printed cookbook does not include a program.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a nonphysical computing concept?

options.A: A fitness tracking app installed on a smartphone
options.B: A smart thermostat that learns user temperature preferences
options.C: A social media platform that connects users across the world
options.D: A photo editing program that applies filters to images

questions.explanation: A nonphysical computing concept is a system or model enabled by computing. A social media platform is a system enabled by computing. A fitness app and photo editing program are nonphysical software. A smart thermostat is a physical innovation.

questions.stimulus: Two programmers are working together on a complex module. Programmer A writes code while Programmer B watches, asks questions, and points out potential issues in real time. After completing the module, they switch roles for the next task.
questions.question: Which of the following best describes the collaboration model being used?

options.A: Iterative development, because the programmers repeatedly refine the same code
options.B: Pair programming, because one programmer writes while the other observes and provides feedback
options.C: Distributed computing, because the two programmers divide the work between two machines
options.D: Modular design, because the programmers break the program into separate modules

questions.explanation: Pair programming is a recognized collaboration model where one programmer writes code while another observes and provides real-time feedback. The switching of roles is consistent with this model.

questions.stimulus: Two programmers are working together on a complex module. Programmer A writes code while Programmer B watches, asks questions, and points out potential issues in real time. After completing the module, they switch roles for the next task.
questions.question: Programmer A argues that Programmer B is not contributing because Programmer B is not writing any code. Which of the following best refutes this claim?

options.A: Programmer B contributes by documenting the code after it is written
options.B: Programmer B contributes by testing the code after the module is complete
options.C: Programmer B actively contributes by catching errors and providing feedback that improves the code in real time
options.D: Programmer B contributes by managing the project timeline while Programmer A focuses on coding

questions.explanation: In pair programming both roles actively contribute. The observer's real-time feedback is a core contribution of the model — catching errors the writer might miss is distinct from testing or documentation which happen after writing.

questions.stimulus: A development team building a navigation app for cyclists uses an online platform where members post code updates, leave comments on each other's work, and suggest improvements asynchronously across different time zones.
questions.question: Which of the following best describes how the team's approach supports collaboration?

options.A: The online platform eliminates the need for the team to practice interpersonal skills because communication is automated
options.B: The online platform allows the team to share code and provide feedback regardless of location or time zone
options.C: The online platform ensures all team members write the same amount of code
options.D: The online platform replaces the need for pair programming by allowing two people to edit the same file

questions.explanation: Online tools support collaboration by allowing programmers to share and provide feedback on ideas and documents. The key benefit described is asynchronous collaboration across locations and time zones.

questions.question: Which of the following lists contains ONLY the interpersonal skills identified as essential for effective collaborative teams?

options.A: Communication, pair programming, conflict resolution, and negotiation
options.B: Communication, consensus building, conflict resolution, and negotiation
options.C: Brainstorming, consensus building, conflict resolution, and user consultation
options.D: Communication, consensus building, iterative development, and negotiation

questions.explanation: Communication, consensus building, conflict resolution, and negotiation are the four interpersonal skills identified for effective collaborative teams. The other options each include a process or model that is not an interpersonal skill.

questions.stimulus: A team is developing a translation app. Before finalizing the design, they survey speakers of 12 different languages, interview immigrants and international students, and conduct usability sessions with non-native speakers of varying fluency levels.
questions.question: Which of the following best explains the purpose of the team's user research approach?

options.A: To identify syntax errors in the app before the coding phase begins
options.B: To ensure the app meets legal requirements for serving international users
options.C: To gather information from potential users so the app can be developed to fully incorporate diverse perspectives
options.D: To replace pair programming with a more efficient feedback method

questions.explanation: Information gathered from potential users can be used to understand the purpose of a program from diverse perspectives and to develop a program that fully incorporates those perspectives.

questions.question: A team develops an automated resume screening tool trained on hiring data from the past 15 years. After deployment, the tool consistently ranks candidates from certain universities significantly higher than equally qualified candidates from other schools. Which TWO of the following best explain this outcome?

options.A: The tool contains a syntax error that causes it to misread university names
options.B: The historical data used to train the tool reflected past hiring preferences, embedding that bias into the tool
options.C: The development team did not include diverse perspectives that might have identified the bias before deployment
options.D: The tool was not tested with enough candidates, causing it to default to random rankings

questions.explanation: The bias comes from two sources: the historical data that reflected past biased hiring practices (B), and the lack of diverse perspectives on the development team that would have identified and questioned that bias before deployment (C).

questions.question: A student creates a program that displays a different greeting depending on the time of day. Which of the following best describes the purpose of this program?

options.A: To store data about the user's preferences in a database
options.B: To solve a problem or pursue an interest through creative expression
options.C: To test whether the computer's clock is functioning correctly
options.D: To demonstrate the use of variables in a programming language

questions.explanation: The purpose of computing innovations is to solve problems or pursue interests through creative expression. A personalized greeting program pursues the interest of providing a customized user experience.

questions.stimulus: A weather app accepts a city name as input and displays the current temperature, humidity, and forecast for that city.
questions.question: Which of the following best describes an event in this program?

options.A: The temperature value stored in the program's memory
options.B: The forecast data retrieved from a weather database
options.C: The user tapping the Search button after typing a city name
options.D: The list of cities the program can retrieve weather data for

questions.explanation: An event is associated with an action and supplies input data to a program. The user tapping the Search button is the action that triggers the program to process the city name input.

questions.stimulus: A weather app accepts a city name as input and displays the current temperature, humidity, and forecast for that city.
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an example of program output produced by this app?

options.A: The current temperature displayed on screen
options.B: The city name typed by the user
options.C: The humidity percentage shown to the user
options.D: The forecast text displayed after the search

questions.explanation: Program outputs are data sent from a program to a device. The city name typed by the user is program input. The temperature, humidity, and forecast are all outputs sent from the program to the user's screen.

questions.question: A programmer is designing a calculator app. The app must correctly handle all possible numeric inputs including very large numbers, decimals, and negative numbers. Which of the following best explains why this requirement exists?

options.A: A program must be written in a language that supports all numeric types
options.B: A program needs to work for a variety of inputs and situations
options.C: A program's output must always be a number when the input is a number
options.D: A program must store all previous inputs in memory for future reference

questions.explanation: A program needs to work for a variety of inputs and situations. A calculator that only works for some numeric inputs would be incomplete.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the difference between a program and a code segment?

options.A: A program is written by one person while a code segment is written collaboratively
options.B: A program performs a complete task when run while a code segment is a collection of statements that is part of a larger program
options.C: A program runs on a computer while a code segment runs on a mobile device
options.D: A program uses variables while a code segment uses only constants

questions.explanation: A program is a collection of program statements that performs a specific task when run. A code segment is a collection of statements that is part of a larger program.

questions.question: A music app plays a song when a user taps a play button and pauses it when the user taps again. Which of the following best describes the programming style used?

options.A: Sequential programming, because the app executes statements in order from top to bottom
options.B: Iterative programming, because the app repeats the same action each time the button is tapped
options.C: Event-driven programming, because program statements execute when triggered by a user action rather than through sequential flow
options.D: Modular programming, because the play and pause functions are separated into different modules

questions.explanation: In event-driven programming, program statements are executed when triggered rather than through sequential flow of control. The user tapping the play button is the event that triggers execution.

questions.question: A navigation app takes a starting location and destination as input and displays a route on a map. Which TWO of the following are examples of program input for this app?

options.A: The map displayed on the user's screen
options.B: The estimated travel time shown after the route is calculated
options.C: The destination address typed by the user
options.D: The user's current GPS location detected by the device

questions.explanation: Program inputs are data sent to a computer for processing. The destination address (C) and GPS location (D) are both inputs. The map display and travel time are outputs sent from the program to the user's screen.

questions.stimulus: A team is developing a fitness tracking app. They release a basic version, gather user feedback, redesign the step-counting feature based on complaints, release an updated version, gather more feedback, and repeat the process several times over six months.
questions.question: Which of the following best describes the development process being used?

options.A: Incremental development, because the team adds new features with each release
options.B: Iterative development, because the team refines and revises the program based on feedback throughout the process
options.C: Sequential development, because the team follows the same steps in the same order each time
options.D: Pair programming, because two developers review each update before it is released

questions.explanation: A development process that is iterative requires refinement and revision based on feedback throughout the process. The team repeatedly revises based on user feedback.

questions.stimulus: A team is building a social media app. They complete and fully test the login system before beginning work on the profile page. Once the profile page works correctly they begin building the feed. They do not start a new feature until the previous one is verified to work.
questions.question: Which of the following best describes the development process being used?

options.A: Iterative development, because the team revisits each feature multiple times
options.B: Exploratory development, because the team does not plan ahead before building each feature
options.C: Incremental development, because the team builds and verifies one piece at a time before adding the next
options.D: Modular development, because the team separates the app into independent modules

questions.explanation: A development process that is incremental breaks the problem into smaller pieces and makes sure each piece works before adding it to the whole.

questions.question: A team is in the investigation phase of developing a new ride-sharing app. Which of the following activities is LEAST consistent with this phase?

options.A: Conducting interviews with frequent ride-sharing users
options.B: Observing how people currently use competing apps
options.C: Writing the code for the payment processing module
options.D: Surveying potential users about their biggest frustrations with existing services

questions.explanation: Investigation methods include surveys, interviews, and direct observations. Writing code belongs to the prototyping phase, not the investigation phase.

questions.question: A programmer adds the following note directly into their code: '# This function calculates the total price including tax'. Which of the following best describes this note?

options.A: A variable that stores the function's return value
options.B: A comment that documents the function's purpose and does not affect how the program runs
options.C: A test case used to verify the function produces the correct output
options.D: A specification that defines the requirements for the program

questions.explanation: Comments are a form of program documentation written into the program to be read by people and do not affect how a program runs.

questions.stimulus: A programmer finds a sorting algorithm online and uses it in their program. They add the following line to their documentation: 'Sorting function adapted from quicksort implementation by J. Smith, available at codeexamples.com'.
questions.question: Which of the following best explains why the programmer added this line?

options.A: To explain how the sorting algorithm works so other programmers can understand the code
options.B: To acknowledge code that was written by another source, as required when using code developed by others
options.C: To document a test case that verifies the sorting function works correctly
options.D: To describe the program specification that required a sorting feature

questions.explanation: It is important to acknowledge any code segments developed by another source. The acknowledgement should be in the program documentation and include the origin or original author's name.

questions.question: Which of the following activities belongs in the DESIGN phase of program development?

options.A: Conducting user interviews to understand the program's requirements
options.B: Running the program with test inputs to check for errors
options.C: Creating diagrams that represent the layout of the user interface
options.D: Releasing a working version of the program to gather user feedback

questions.explanation: The design phase may include brainstorming, planning, organizing into modules, creation of UI layout diagrams, and development of a testing strategy. User interviews are investigation, running tests is testing, and releasing to users is prototyping.

questions.question: Which TWO of the following are effective methods of investigation during the development of a new program?

options.A: Writing comments that explain what each code segment does
options.B: Conducting surveys to collect data from potential users
options.C: Organizing the program into separate modules and functional components
options.D: Directly observing how people currently perform the task the program will automate

questions.explanation: Investigation methods include collecting data through surveys and direct observations. Writing comments is documentation and organizing into modules belongs to the design phase.

questions.stimulus: A programmer writes a program that is intended to calculate the average of a list of numbers. When run with the input [10, 20, 30], the program displays 25 instead of 20.
questions.question: Which of the following best describes the type of error in this program?

options.A: Syntax error, because the program does not follow the rules of the programming language
options.B: Run-time error, because the program crashes when given this input
options.C: Overflow error, because the numbers exceed the defined range of values
options.D: Logic error, because the program runs but produces an incorrect result

questions.explanation: A logic error is a mistake in the algorithm or program that causes it to behave incorrectly or unexpectedly. The program runs without crashing but produces the wrong answer.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the error in this code segment?

options.A: Logic error, because the calculation produces the wrong result
options.B: Run-time error, because the program will crash when it reaches the DISPLAY statement
options.C: Syntax error, because the variable name rezult does not match the variable result that was defined
options.D: Overflow error, because the result of 10 + 5 exceeds the defined range of values

questions.explanation: A syntax error is a mistake where the rules of the programming language are not followed. Using rezult instead of result violates the rule that variable names must be consistent.

questions.question: A program that divides two numbers crashes when the user enters 0 as the denominator. Which of the following best describes this type of error?

options.A: Logic error, because dividing by zero produces an incorrect mathematical result
options.B: Syntax error, because division by zero violates the rules of the programming language
options.C: Run-time error, because the error occurs during the execution of the program
options.D: Overflow error, because zero is outside the defined range of values for a denominator

questions.explanation: A run-time error is a mistake that occurs during execution. Division by zero does not prevent the program from starting but causes it to crash when that line executes.

questions.stimulus: A programmer is trying to find an error in a procedure that calculates shipping costs. She manually traces through each line of the code using a specific test input, tracking the value of each variable at each step.
questions.question: Which of the following best describes the error-finding technique being used?

options.A: Adding extra output statements to print variable values during execution
options.B: Using a debugger to pause execution at specific lines
options.C: Hand tracing, by manually tracking variable values through each step of the code
options.D: Using a test case to compare expected and actual output

questions.explanation: Hand tracing involves manually stepping through the code and tracking variable values at each step. The programmer is hand tracing, not using a debugger or test case.

questions.question: A programmer is testing a program that accepts a user's age as input and must work correctly for all valid ages. Which of the following sets of test inputs would be MOST effective for verifying the program works correctly?
questions.explanation: Defined inputs used to test a program should demonstrate expected outcomes at or just beyond the extremes of input data. Testing with boundary values (0, 1 for minimum and 99, 100 for maximum) is most effective for catching edge case errors.

questions.question: A program that tracks a company's annual revenue stores the value as an integer. At the end of a record-breaking year, the program suddenly displays a negative revenue figure even though all individual transactions were positive. Which of the following best explains this behavior?

options.A: A logic error caused the program to subtract transactions instead of adding them
options.B: A syntax error caused the program to misread the transaction data
options.C: An overflow error occurred because the revenue value exceeded the defined range of values for the integer type
options.D: A run-time error caused the program to crash and reset the revenue to a negative default value

questions.explanation: An overflow error occurs when a computer attempts to handle a number outside the defined range of values. When an integer exceeds its maximum value it wraps around to a negative number, which explains the unexpected negative result.

questions.question: Which TWO of the following are effective ways to find and correct errors in a program?

options.A: Adding extra output statements to display variable values at key points during execution
options.B: Rewriting the program in a different programming language
options.C: Using a debugger to pause execution and inspect variable values
options.D: Increasing the number of programmers working on the program

questions.explanation: Adding extra output statements and using debuggers are both identified as effective ways to find and correct errors. Rewriting in a different language does not fix logic errors, and adding more programmers does not directly help identify existing errors.
